description:
## Overview

Manage the device's WiFi and Ethernet connections. The device supports both interfaces simultaneously for flexible networking.

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `get_wifi` | Retrieve WiFi status including connected SSID, IP, signal strength, security type |
| `set_wifi` | Configure WiFi access point, security (WPA2/WPA3/Enterprise), and IP settings |
| `delete_wifi_profile` | Remove a saved WiFi profile by SSID |
| `get_eth` | Retrieve Ethernet status including link state, speed, IP address, security |

## Features

- WiFi profiles stored on device
- Automatic reconnection after reboot
- IPv4 DHCP addressing
- WPA2/WPA3 security support

post.description:
The delete_wifi_profile command removes a saved Wi-Fi profile from the reader.

When you run this command, the profile identified by ESSID is deleted from stored Wi-Fi configurations.

Use this command to:
- Remove obsolete or incorrect Wi-Fi profiles
- Clean up profile lists before adding updated configurations
- Manage active and fallback network profile sets

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Wi-Fi Profile Deletion |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related commands | [set_wifi](#op-set-wifi), [get_wifi](#op-get-wifi), [delete_certificate](#op-delete-certificate) |
| Parameters | command, requestId, wifiProfileInfo |
| Supported Identifier | essid |

command.description: Specifies the operation being performed, in this case, deleting wifi profile.

requestId.description: A unique identifier for the request, allowing tracking and debugging of the operation.

wifiProfileInfo.title: wifiProfileInfo
wifiProfileInfo.description: Object containing the details of the wifi profile to be deleted, including its ESSID and security type.

essid.description: SSID of the wifi profile to be deleted

Delete wifi profile.description: Payload to delete a saved WiFi profile

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the delete_wifi_profile request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 2 — Invalid payload
- 15 — WIFI Error - SSID not found
- 16 — WIFI Error - Cannot delete active SSID
- 17 — WIFI Error - SSID missed

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

response.description: WIFI Error - Cannot delete active SSID

x-error-codes.cause: The JSON payload is malformed or contains invalid field values
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Validate the payload against the command schema and correct any errors

x-error-codes.cause: The specified Wi-Fi SSID does not exist in saved profiles
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Verify the SSID name is correct and that the profile has been saved

x-error-codes.description: WIFI Error - Cannot delete active SSID
x-error-codes.cause: The Wi-Fi profile being deleted is the currently connected network
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Disconnect from the network or connect to a different SSID before deleting

x-error-codes.cause: The required SSID field is missing from the payload
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Include the SSID field in the command payload

post.description:
The get_eth command retrieves Ethernet interface configuration and connection state from the reader.

This command returns:
- Ethernet interface status and link state details
- DHCP enablement and IPv4 addressing information
- Interface-level network parameters and connectivity metadata
- Response metadata for command execution

Use this command to:
- Verify Ethernet connectivity and link speed
- Check interface status and network addressing
- Confirm DHCP-based IPv4 network configuration
- Troubleshoot wired network availability

No additional command-specific payload fields are required beyond the standard request envelope fields.

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Ethernet Configuration Query |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_wifi](#op-get-wifi), [get_config](#op-get-config), [set_config](#op-set-config) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId |
| Supported Operations | Retrieve Ethernet network configuration and interface status |
| Supported Response Sections | ethConfig, response |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Command name for requesting Ethernet configuration details.

requestId.description: Unique request identifier used to correlate this command with its response.

command.description: The command issued by the client, indicating the operation to retrieve Ethernet configuration and status information.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: The version of the API being used for the request.

ethConfig.title: ethConfig
ethConfig.description: Ethernet interface details, including status, link information, and IPv4 configuration.

interfaceDetails.description: Ethernet interface details including link state, addressing, and optional security settings.

macAddress.description: MAC address of the network interface.

status.description: Current status of the network link.

ipAddress.description: IPv4 address of the network interface.

subnetMask.description: Subnet mask for the IPv4 configuration.

gateway.description: Gateway address for the IPv4 configuration.

enableDhcp.description: Indicates if DHCP is enabled for IPv4 configuration.

dnsServer.description: DNS server address for the IPv4 configuration.

domainName.description: Domain name for the IPv4 configuration.

securityStatus.description: Indicates if security features are enabled.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 3 — Not able to retrieve information

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

Ethernet config info.description: Response with ethernet interface configuration details

Ethernet interface disabled.description: Response when ethernet interface is disabled on the device

x-error-codes.cause: The device could not gather the requested information at this time
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Retry the command after a short delay; if persistent, reboot the device

post.description:
The get_wifi command retrieves saved and active Wi-Fi configuration details from the reader.

This command returns:
- Saved and active Wi-Fi profile configuration details
- Interface network settings and DHCP addressing state
- Access point and security configuration parameters
- Response metadata for command execution

Use this command to:
- Verify connected and saved Wi-Fi profiles
- Check interface addressing and DHCP state
- Validate security setup and profile preference behavior
- Troubleshoot Wi-Fi availability and profile issues

No additional command-specific payload fields are required beyond the standard request envelope fields.

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Wi-Fi Configuration Retrieval |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [set_wifi](#op-set-wifi), [delete_wifi_profile](#op-delete-wifi-profile), [get_eth](#op-get-eth), [get_config](#op-get-config) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId |
| Supported Operations | Retrieve Wi-Fi interface and profile configuration details |
| Supported Response Sections | wifiProfiles, response |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Command name for requesting Wi-Fi configuration details.

requestId.description: Unique request identifier used to correlate this command with its response.

command.description: The command issued by the client, indicating the operation to retrieve Wi-Fi configuration and status information.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

wifiProfiles.title: wifiProfiles
wifiProfiles.description: Object containing an array of Wi-Fi configuration profiles, each with detailed information about the Wi-Fi interface, access point, IP configuration, and security settings.

wifiConfig.description: List of Wi-Fi profile entries with interface and security details.

items.title: wifiConfig

interfaceDetails.description: Wi-Fi interface information and associated network configuration.

interfaceName.description: The name of the network interface (e.g., wlan0).

status.description: The status of the network interface, either "ENABLED" or "DISABLED".

macAddress.description: The MAC address of the network interface.

accessPoint.description: Details of the access point profile associated with this Wi-Fi entry.

essid.description: The ESSID (network name) of the access point.

status.description: The connection status to the access point, either "CONNECTED" or "DISCONNECTED".

securityType.description: The security type used by the access point.

isPreferred.description: Indicates whether this access point is preferred for connection.

autoConn.description: This field is deprecated and kept for backward compatibility.

ipv4Configuration.description: IPv4 settings currently applied to the Wi-Fi interface.

ipAddress.description: The IPv4 address of the device.

subnetMask.description: The subnet mask of the network.

enableDhcp.description: Indicates if DHCP is enabled for IPv4 configuration.

dnsServer.description: The IPv4 address of the DNS server.

domainName.description: The domain name of the network.

securityDetails.description: Security configuration details based on the selected Wi-Fi security type.

WPA2Personal.description: WPA2-Personal security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

password.description: The password used for WPA2-Personal security.

WPA3Personal.description: WPA3-Personal security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

password.description: The password used for WPA3-Personal security.

WPA2Enterprise.description: WPA2-Enterprise security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

innerAuthentication.description: The inner authentication protocol for WPA2-Enterprise.

identity.description: The identity used for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity used for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

items.description: One certificate key or value pair for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate (e.g., ca_cert, client_key).

WPA3Enterprise.description: WPA3-Enterprise security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

innerAuthentication.description: The inner authentication protocol for WPA3-Enterprise.

identity.description: The identity used for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity used for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

items.description: One certificate key or value pair for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate (e.g., ca_cert, client_key).

OWEPublic.description: OWE public-network security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

authentication.description: The authentication method for OWE-Public access.

items.description: Wi-Fi network configuration — interface status (enabled/disabled), connected access point (SSID, signal, security type), IP addressing (IPv4, DHCP or static), and enterprise security credentials (WPA2/WPA3 Personal or Enterprise, certificates).

response.title: response
response.description: Object containing the response code and description for the get_wifi command.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 3 — Not able to retrieve information

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

WPA2 Personal connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to WPA2 Personal network

WPA2 Enterprise connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to WPA2 Enterprise network with certificates

WPA3 Enterprise connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to WPA3 Enterprise network with advanced security

Open network connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to open/unsecured network

WiFi interface disabled.description: Response when WiFi interface is disabled on the device

x-error-codes.cause: The device could not gather the requested information at this time
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Retry the command after a short delay; if persistent, reboot the device

post.description:
The set_wifi command configures or updates Wi-Fi interface and access point profile settings on the reader.

This command allows you to configure:
- Wi-Fi profile create and modify operations
- Interface enablement and preferred connection behavior
- Access point ESSID and connection settings
- Security type and authentication details for protected networks

Use this command to:
- Provision new Wi-Fi profiles during device onboarding
- Update existing Wi-Fi settings for network changes
- Control security and connection behavior for wireless access

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Wi-Fi Configuration |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_wifi](#op-get-wifi), [delete_wifi_profile](#op-delete-wifi-profile), [set_config](#op-set-config), [install_certificate](#op-install-certificate) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId, wifiConfig |
| Supported Operations | CREATE, MODIFY |
| Supported Types | Security types: WPA2Personal, WPA3Personal, WPA2Enterprise, WPA3Enterprise, OWEPublic, Open; enterprise authentication: tls, ttls, peap |
| Supported Profiles | ESSID-based Wi-Fi access point profiles with optional preferred and auto-connect behavior |
| Supported Protocols | Not applicable |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

wifiConfig contains the configuration and operation details required by the command.

command.description: Specifies the operation being performed, in this case, setting the wifi configuration.

requestId.description: A unique identifier for the request, allowing tracking and debugging of the operation.

wifiConfig.title: wifiConfig
wifiConfig.description: Object containing the Wi-Fi configuration details to be set on the device, including interface settings, access point information, and security configurations.

interfaceName.description: The name of the network interface for which the Wi-Fi configuration is being set.

enableInterface.description: Specifies whether the network interface should be enabled or disabled.

operation.description: The operation to be performed on the Wi-Fi configuration. It can either create a new configuration or modify an existing one.

accessPoint.description: Configuration details of the Wi-Fi access point.

connect.description: Indicates whether to connect to this profile. If true, disconnects the currently connected profile and connects to the specified one; if false, just saves the profile.

isPreferred.description: Marks this profile as the preferred one to always connect to when available.

autoConn.description: Reserved for backward compatibility. It will be removed in the future.

essid.description: The ESSID (network name) of the access point.

enableSecurity.description: Indicates whether security is enabled for this access point.

ipAddress.description: The IPv4 address to assign to the interface.

enableDhcp.description: Specifies whether DHCP is enabled for automatic IP address assignment.

dnsServer.description: The IPv4 address of the DNS server.

domainName.description: The domain name for the network.

security.description: Security configuration for the access point.

securityType.description: The security type for the access point, specifying the authentication and encryption method.

WPA2Enterprise.description: WPA2-Enterprise security configuration with optional certificate parameters.

innerAuthentication.description: Inner authentication method to be used within the outer authentication protocol.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

certificate.description: List of certificates used for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate to be used.

value.description: The name of the specific certificate.

WPA3Enterprise.description: WPA3-Enterprise security configuration with optional certificate parameters.

innerAuthentication.description: Inner authentication method to be used within the outer authentication protocol.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

certificate.description: List of certificates used for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate to be used.

value.description: The name of the specific certificate.

Create WPA2 personal.description: Payload to create a new WiFi profile with WPA2 Personal security

Modify WPA2 personal.description: Payload to modify an existing WiFi profile password

Set preferred AP.description: Payload to set an access point as preferred

Create WPA2 enterprise.description: Payload to create a WiFi profile with WPA2 Enterprise security and certificates

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the set_wifi request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 2 — Invalid payload
- 15 — WIFI Error - SSID not found
- 17 — WIFI Error - SSID missed
- 18 — WIFI Error - SSID already exist
- 19 — WIFI Error - SSID count overflow
- 20 — Wifi is not supported
- 23 — Invalid enum value

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

x-error-codes.cause: The JSON payload is malformed or contains invalid field values
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Validate the payload against the command schema and correct any errors

x-error-codes.cause: The specified Wi-Fi SSID does not exist in saved profiles
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Verify the SSID name is correct and that the profile has been saved

x-error-codes.cause: The required SSID field is missing from the payload
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Include the SSID field in the command payload

x-error-codes.cause: A Wi-Fi profile with this SSID is already saved on the device
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Delete the existing profile first or update it instead of creating a new one

x-error-codes.cause: The maximum number of saved Wi-Fi profiles has been reached
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Delete an unused Wi-Fi profile before adding a new one

x-error-codes.cause: The device hardware does not have a Wi-Fi interface
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Use Ethernet or another supported interface for network connectivity

x-error-codes.cause: A field value does not match any of the allowed enum options
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the schema for allowed values and correct the field
